tasks.title: Async producer-consumer with backpressure
tasks.description: Write a Python async solution with: `AsyncQueue` class that wraps asyncio.Queue with a max size, `producer(queue, items)` coroutine that puts items into the queue with backpressure, and `consumer(queue, results)` coroutine that processes items and appends to results. Both should work correctly with asyncio.gather. The queue should block producers when full.
tests: import asyncio; results = []; queue = AsyncQueue(maxsize=2); asyncio.run(asyncio.gather(producer(queue, [1,2,3]), consumer(queue, results))); assert sorted(results) == [1,2,3]
import asyncio; results = []; queue = AsyncQueue(maxsize=1); asyncio.run(asyncio.gather(producer(queue, list(range(5))), consumer(queue, results))); assert len(results) == 5
hidden_tests: import asyncio; q = AsyncQueue(maxsize=3); assert q.maxsize == 3
import asyncio; results = []; queue = AsyncQueue(maxsize=10); asyncio.run(asyncio.gather(producer(queue, []), consumer(queue, results))); assert results == []

tasks.title: Thread-safe bounded counter
tasks.description: Write a Python class `BoundedCounter` with `__init__(self, max_val: int)`, `increment(self) -> bool` (thread-safe, returns True if incremented, False if already at max), `decrement(self) -> bool` (returns True if decremented, False if already at 0), and `value(self) -> int`. Must be safe under concurrent access from multiple threads.
tests: c = BoundedCounter(3); assert c.increment() is True; assert c.increment() is True; assert c.value() == 2
c = BoundedCounter(1); c.increment(); assert c.increment() is False; assert c.value() == 1
c = BoundedCounter(5); assert c.decrement() is False; assert c.value() == 0
hidden_tests: import threading; c = BoundedCounter(100); threads = [threading.Thread(target=c.increment) for _ in range(50)]; [t.start() for t in threads]; [t.join() for t in threads]; assert c.value() == 50
c = BoundedCounter(2); c.increment(); c.increment(); c.decrement(); assert c.value() == 1

tasks.title: Custom context manager with retry
tasks.description: Write a Python class `RetryContext` that acts as a context manager and decorator. `__init__(self, max_retries: int, exceptions: tuple = (Exception,), delay: float = 0.0)`. When used as a context manager or decorator, it retries the block/function up to max_retries times on the specified exceptions. After exhausting retries it re-raises the last exception.
tests:
calls = []; ctx = RetryContext(3)
with ctx:
    calls.append(1)
assert calls == [1]

calls = [0]
def flaky():
    calls[0] += 1
    if calls[0] < 3:
        raise ValueError('not yet')
RetryContext(5)(flaky)()
assert calls[0] == 3

import pytest
ctx = RetryContext(2, exceptions=(ValueError,))
try:
    with ctx:
        raise ValueError('fail')
except ValueError:
    pass

tasks.title: Implement a simple event loop dispatcher
tasks.description: Write a Python class `EventDispatcher` with `subscribe(event: str, handler: callable) -> None`, `unsubscribe(event: str, handler: callable) -> None`, `emit(event: str, *args, **kwargs) -> list` (calls all handlers, collects return values). Thread-safe. Handlers are called in subscription order.
tests: d = EventDispatcher(); results = []; d.subscribe('click', lambda x: results.append(x)); d.emit('click', 42); assert results == [42]
d = EventDispatcher(); h = lambda: 'hello'; d.subscribe('x', h); d.unsubscribe('x', h); d.emit('x'); assert d.emit('x') == []
d = EventDispatcher(); d.subscribe('e', lambda: 1); d.subscribe('e', lambda: 2); r = d.emit('e'); assert r == [1, 2]
hidden_tests: import threading; d = EventDispatcher(); calls = []; d.subscribe('t', lambda: calls.append(1)); threads = [threading.Thread(target=d.emit, args=('t',)) for _ in range(10)]; [t.start() for t in threads]; [t.join() for t in threads]; assert len(calls) == 10

tasks.title: Trie with autocomplete
tasks.description: Write a Python class `Trie` with `insert(word: str) -> None`, `search(word: str) -> bool` (exact match), `starts_with(prefix: str) -> bool`, and `autocomplete(prefix: str) -> list[str]` (returns all words with the given prefix, sorted alphabetically).
tests: t = Trie(); t.insert('apple'); assert t.search('apple') is True; assert t.search('app') is False
t = Trie(); t.insert('apple'); assert t.starts_with('app') is True; assert t.starts_with('xyz') is False
t = Trie(); t.insert('apple'); t.insert('app'); t.insert('application'); result = t.autocomplete('app'); assert sorted(result) == ['app', 'apple', 'application']
hidden_tests: t = Trie(); t.insert('hello'); t.insert('world'); assert t.autocomplete('') == ['hello', 'world']
